metadata.title: FAO-56 Table 12 - Single (time-averaged) crop coefficients Kc and mean maximum plant heights
metadata.source: Allen, R.G., Pereira, L.S., Raes, D., Smith, M. (1998). FAO Irrigation and Drainage Paper No. 56. Crop Evapotranspiration: Guidelines for computing crop water requirements. Rome: FAO.

field_definitions.kc.ini: Crop coefficient during initial growth stage
field_definitions.kc.end: Crop coefficient at the end of the late-season stage

value_encoding.range: Object {low, high} when FAO-56 specifies a range (e.g., {"low": 1.0, "high": 1.15})

applicability_notes: Values are for non-stressed crops under excellent agronomic and water management conditions.
For RHmin different from 45% or u2 different from 2 m/s, Kc_mid and Kc_end must be adjusted using FAO-56 Eq. 62 and 65.
For high-frequency wetting (drip or daily rainfall), Kc_ini may approach 1.0-1.2; consider Eq. 7-3 (Annex 7) or dual coefficient (Kcb + Ke).

sweet_peppers_bell.name: Sweet Peppers (bell)
sweet_peppers_bell.notes: Footnote 2: When grown on stalks reaching 1.5-2 m height, increase Kc_mid to 1.15.

tomato.name: Tomato
tomato.notes: Footnote 2: When grown on stalks reaching 1.5-2 m height, increase Kc_mid to 1.20.

cucumber_fresh_market.name: Cucumber (Fresh Market)
cucumber_fresh_market.notes: Footnote 2: When grown on stalks reaching 1.5-2 m height, increase Kc_mid to 1.15.

cassava_year_1.name: Cassava (year 1)
cassava_year_1.notes: Footnote 3: Mid-season values assume non-stressed conditions during/following the rainy season. Kc_end accounts for dormancy during the dry season.

cassava_year_2.name: Cassava (year 2)
cassava_year_2.notes: Footnote 3: Mid-season values assume non-stressed conditions during/following the rainy season.

potato.name: Potato
potato.notes: Footnote 4: Kc_end is about 0.40 for long-season potatoes with vine kill.

sugar_beet.name: Sugar Beet
sugar_beet.notes: Footnote 5: Kc_end of 0.70 is for no irrigation during the last month. With irrigation or significant rain in the last month, Kc_end can reach up to 1.0.

beans_green.name: Beans, green
beans_green.notes: Footnote 2: When grown on stalks reaching 1.5-2 m height, increase Kc_mid to 1.15.

beans_dry_pulses.name: Beans, dry and Pulses
beans_dry_pulses.notes: Footnote 2: When grown on stalks reaching 1.5-2 m height, increase Kc_mid to 1.20.

fababean_fresh.name: Fababean / Broad bean (Fresh)
fababean_fresh.notes: Footnote 2: When grown on stalks reaching 1.5-2 m height, increase Kc_mid to 1.20.

fababean_dry_seed.name: Fababean / Broad bean (Dry/Seed)
fababean_dry_seed.notes: Footnote 2: When grown on stalks reaching 1.5-2 m height, increase Kc_mid to 1.20.

green_gram_cowpeas.name: Green Gram and Cowpeas
green_gram_cowpeas.notes: Footnote 6: First Kc_end value (0.60) is for harvested fresh; second value (0.35) is for harvested dry.

peas_fresh.name: Peas (Fresh)
peas_fresh.notes: Footnote 2: When grown on stalks reaching 1.5-2 m height, increase Kc_mid to 1.20.

asparagus.name: Asparagus
asparagus.notes: Footnote 7: Kc usually remains at Kc_ini during harvest of the spears (sparse ground cover). Kc_mid value is for following regrowth after harvest termination.

sisal.name: Sisal
sisal.notes: Footnote 8: Kc for sisal depends on planting density and water management (e.g., intentional moisture stress).

rapeseed_canola.name: Rapeseed, Canola
rapeseed_canola.notes: Footnote 9: Lower Kc_mid values are for rainfed crops with less dense plant populations.

safflower.name: Safflower
safflower.notes: Footnote 9: Lower Kc_mid values are for rainfed crops with less dense plant populations.

sunflower.name: Sunflower
sunflower.notes: Footnote 9: Lower Kc_mid values are for rainfed crops with less dense plant populations.

spring_wheat.name: Spring Wheat
spring_wheat.notes: Footnote 10: Higher Kc_end value is for hand-harvested crops.

winter_wheat_frozen_soils.name: Winter Wheat (with frozen soils)
winter_wheat_frozen_soils.notes: Footnote 10: Higher Kc_end value is for hand-harvested crops.

winter_wheat_non_frozen_soils.name: Winter Wheat (with non-frozen soils)
winter_wheat_non_frozen_soils.notes: Footnote 10: Higher Kc_end value is for hand-harvested crops.

maize_field_grain.name: Maize, Field (grain) / Field corn
maize_field_grain.notes: Footnote 11: Kc_end of 0.60 is for harvest at high grain moisture; 0.35 is for harvest after complete field drying (~18% moisture, wet mass basis).

maize_sweet.name: Maize, Sweet (sweet corn)
maize_sweet.notes: Footnote 12: Kc_end of 1.05 applies if harvested fresh for human consumption. Use Kc_end for field maize if sweet maize is allowed to mature and dry in the field.

alfalfa_hay_averaged.name: Alfalfa Hay (averaged cutting effects)
alfalfa_hay_averaged.notes: Footnote 13: Kc_mid is an overall average that averages Kc for both before and following cuttings. Applied from after first development period until before the last late-season period.

alfalfa_hay_individual_cutting.name: Alfalfa Hay (individual cutting periods)
alfalfa_hay_individual_cutting.notes: Footnote 14: Values represent immediately following cutting (Kc_ini), at full cover (Kc_mid), and immediately before cutting (Kc_end). Growing season is described as a series of cutting periods.

bermuda_hay_averaged.name: Bermuda hay (averaged cutting effects)
bermuda_hay_averaged.notes: Footnote 13: Kc_mid is an overall average across cuttings.

clover_hay_berseem_averaged.name: Clover hay, Berseem (averaged cutting effects)
clover_hay_berseem_averaged.notes: Footnote 13: Kc_mid is an overall average across cuttings.

clover_hay_berseem_individual_cutting.name: Clover hay, Berseem (individual cutting periods)
clover_hay_berseem_individual_cutting.notes: Footnote 14: Values for immediately after cutting / full cover / before cutting respectively.

sudan_grass_hay_averaged.name: Sudan Grass hay (annual, averaged cutting effects)
sudan_grass_hay_averaged.notes: Footnote 14: Average Kc across cutting cycles.

sudan_grass_hay_individual_cutting.name: Sudan Grass hay (individual cutting periods)
sudan_grass_hay_individual_cutting.notes: Footnote 14: Values for immediately after cutting / full cover / before cutting respectively.

turf_grass_cool_season.name: Turf grass (cool season)
turf_grass_cool_season.scientific_name: Poa pratensis / Lolium spp. / Festuca spp.
turf_grass_cool_season.notes: Footnote 15: Cool season varieties include bluegrass, ryegrass, fescue. The 0.95 represents 6-8 cm mowing height under general turf conditions; reduce by 0.10 with careful water management and slower growth tolerance.

turf_grass_warm_season.name: Turf grass (warm season)
turf_grass_warm_season.notes: Footnote 15: Warm season varieties include bermuda grass and St. Augustine grass.

pineapple_bare_soil.name: Pineapple (bare soil)
pineapple_bare_soil.notes: Footnote 16: Pineapple has very low transpiration (CAM stomatal closure during the day). Most ETc is soil evaporation. Values assume 50% black plastic mulch and sprinkler irrigation. For drip beneath plastic mulch, reduce Kc by 0.10.

pineapple_with_grass_cover.name: Pineapple (with grass cover)
pineapple_with_grass_cover.notes: Footnote 16: See pineapple_bare_soil for context on CAM physiology and management assumptions.

tea_shaded.name: Tea (shaded)
tea_shaded.notes: Footnote 17: Includes the water requirements of the shade trees.

almonds_no_ground_cover.name: Almonds (no ground cover)
almonds_no_ground_cover.notes: Footnote 18: Kc_end represents Kc prior to leaf drop. After leaf drop, Kc_end ~0.20 for bare/dry soil or dead ground cover, and 0.50-0.80 for actively growing ground cover (Chapter 11).

apples_cherries_pears_no_cover_killing_f.name: Apples, Cherries, Pears (no ground cover, killing frost)
apples_cherries_pears_no_cover_killing_f.scientific_name: Malus domestica / Prunus avium / Pyrus communis
apples_cherries_pears_no_cover_killing_f.notes: Footnote 18: Kc_end is prior to leaf drop. Footnote 19: Refer to FAO-56 Eq. 94, 97 or 98 for immature stands.

apples_cherries_pears_no_cover_no_frost.name: Apples, Cherries, Pears (no ground cover, no frosts)
apples_cherries_pears_no_cover_no_frost.scientific_name: Malus domestica / Prunus avium / Pyrus communis
apples_cherries_pears_no_cover_no_frost.notes: Footnote 18: Kc_end is prior to leaf drop.

apples_cherries_pears_active_cover_killi.name: Apples, Cherries, Pears (active ground cover, killing frost)
apples_cherries_pears_active_cover_killi.scientific_name: Malus domestica / Prunus avium / Pyrus communis
apples_cherries_pears_active_cover_killi.notes: Footnote 18: Kc_end is prior to leaf drop.

apples_cherries_pears_active_cover_no_fr.name: Apples, Cherries, Pears (active ground cover, no frosts)
apples_cherries_pears_active_cover_no_fr.scientific_name: Malus domestica / Prunus avium / Pyrus communis
apples_cherries_pears_active_cover_no_fr.notes: Footnote 18: Kc_end is prior to leaf drop.

apricots_peaches_stone_no_cover_killing_.name: Apricots, Peaches, Stone Fruit (no ground cover, killing frost)
apricots_peaches_stone_no_cover_killing_.scientific_name: Prunus armeniaca / Prunus persica / Prunus spp.
apricots_peaches_stone_no_cover_killing_.notes: Footnotes 18, 20: Stone fruit category includes peaches, apricots, pears, plums, pecans.

apricots_peaches_stone_no_cover_no_frost.name: Apricots, Peaches, Stone Fruit (no ground cover, no frosts)
apricots_peaches_stone_no_cover_no_frost.scientific_name: Prunus armeniaca / Prunus persica / Prunus spp.

apricots_peaches_stone_active_cover_kill.name: Apricots, Peaches, Stone Fruit (active ground cover, killing frost)
apricots_peaches_stone_active_cover_kill.scientific_name: Prunus armeniaca / Prunus persica / Prunus spp.

apricots_peaches_stone_active_cover_no_f.name: Apricots, Peaches, Stone Fruit (active ground cover, no frosts)
apricots_peaches_stone_active_cover_no_f.scientific_name: Prunus armeniaca / Prunus persica / Prunus spp.

citrus_no_cover_70_canopy.name: Citrus (no ground cover, 70% canopy)
citrus_no_cover_70_canopy.notes: Footnote 21: Compute via Eq. 98 with Kc_min=0.15 and Kc_full=0.75/0.70/0.75 for ini/mid/end. For humid/subhumid climates, increase Kc by 0.1-0.2 (Rogers et al. 1983).

citrus_active_cover_70_canopy.name: Citrus (with active ground cover or weeds, 70% canopy)
citrus_active_cover_70_canopy.notes: Footnote 22: Compute via Kc = fc*Kc_ngc + (1-fc)*Kc_cover. For humid/subhumid climates, increase Kc by 0.1-0.2.

citrus_active_cover_50_canopy.name: Citrus (with active ground cover or weeds, 50% canopy)

citrus_active_cover_20_canopy.name: Citrus (with active ground cover or weeds, 20% canopy)

conifer_trees.name: Conifer Trees
conifer_trees.notes: Footnote 23: Conifers exhibit substantial stomatal control due to reduced aerodynamic resistance. Kc can easily reduce below these values, which represent well-watered conditions for large forests.

olives.name: Olives (40-60% ground cover by canopy)
olives.notes: Footnote 24: Coefficients represent 40-60% ground cover. For Spanish 60% cover orchards (Pastor & Orgaz 1994), monthly Kc Jan-Dec: 0.50, 0.50, 0.65, 0.60, 0.55, 0.50, 0.45, 0.45, 0.55, 0.60, 0.65, 0.50.

walnut_orchard.name: Walnut Orchard
walnut_orchard.notes: Footnote 18: Kc_end represents Kc prior to leaf drop.

open_water_shallow_subhumid_or_tropical.name: Open Water (< 2 m depth, or in subhumid climates / tropics)

open_water_deep_temperate.name: Open Water (> 5 m depth, clear, temperate climate)
open_water_deep_temperate.notes: Footnote 25: For deep water in temperate latitudes with large annual temperature changes. Initial/peak ET is low as radiation is absorbed into the water body. In fall/winter, heat release from the water body increases evaporation above grass values. Kc_mid corresponds to thermal energy gain phase; Kc_end to thermal energy release. Use with caution.
